violations.text: The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by new droppings. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/20/2026
A dead mouse was observed in the corner of the hostess area. Mouse was removed at the time of inspection. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/20/2026
A hole was observed by the bar and by the hostess area. Holes to be sealed. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/20/2026
A dead mouse was observed at the time of inspection. PIC disposed of mouse at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/05/2026

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by accumulation of droppings. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/05/2026
A hole was observed in the wall next to the bar area. Per PIC, hole will be fixed tomorrow. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/05/2026

All foods were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 12/22/2025
2 dead rodents were observed in the bar area. Dead pests shall be removed from premises. PIC removed pests. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/22/2025

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by accumulation of droppings. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 12/22/2025
Several food containers were observed uncovered in the walk-in. Food shall be protected from contamination. New Violation. Correct By: 12/22/2025
The prep sink faucet was observed with a leak. Leak to be fixed. New Violation. Correct By: 12/22/2025
The non-food contact surfaces of the shelving near the hostess area and in the bar area have an accumulation of droppings and other miscellaneous debris. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/22/2025
A hole was observed in the wall at the bar area. Wall to be sealed. The FRP was observed peeling away from the wall near the prep sink. Wall to be repaired. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/05/2026

Several areas in the kitchen has an accumulation of grease and other debris. The bar area had an accumulation of droppings behind coolers and dish machine. Areas to be cleaned. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/22/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the interior of the refrigeration units were observed with an accumulation of food debris. Non-food contact surfaces to be cleaned as often as necessary. New Violation. Correct By: 09/02/2025

The floors in the closet near the door to the outside was observed with an accumulation of old rodent droppings. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 09/02/2025

Raw animal foods were not stored properly to prevent cross-contamination. Except when combined as ingredients, different types of raw animal foods must be separated from each other during storage by using separate equipment or arranging each type of food in equipment so that cross contamination of one type with another is prevented. Chicken was observed stored over beef. PIC relocated chicken. Guidance document provided. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/19/2025

Food contact surfaces of ice machine were observed with an accumulation of bio-film. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Ice machine was turned off. Ice was voluntarily disposed of. Bagged ice to be used until ice machine is thoroughly cleaned and sanitized. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/19/2025

Sour cream and whipped cream was observed held above 41 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Food was disposed of. All other food relocated to walk-In. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/19/2025

The consumer advisory provided does not disclose the burgers on the menu that are raw or undercooked. The consumer advisory symbol and symbol on the food must match. New menus to be created. New Violation. Correct By: 08/19/2025
Working containers used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. Bottle labeled. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/19/2025

Quaternary ammonium sanitizer solution was in excess of 400 parts per million (ppm). A quaternary ammonia sanitizer solution applied to food contact surfaces must be applied at the strength as indicated on the manufacturers' label. Bottle was emptied and filled with proper ppm solution. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/19/2025

The presence of flying insects is not controlled as evidenced by accumulation of flying insects throughout the establishment. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 08/19/2025
The pepsi cooler and gasket of prep unit was observed in poor repair. Pepsi cooler to not be used for TCS foods until repaired. Unit was observed iced up. New Violation. Correct By: 08/19/2025
The facility is lacking thermo labels or similar internal temperature indicating device for the dish machine at the dish machine. Per PIC, order will be placed. New Violation. Correct By: 08/19/2025
The non-food contact surfaces of the the shelving and inside of refrigeration units was observed with an accumulation of food debris/grease. Areas to be cleaned. New Violation. Correct By: 08/19/2025

The the floors in the area next to walk-in and in storage closet has an accumulation of old rodent droppings. The floor in the kitchen has an accumulation of food and grease. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 08/19/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine and slicer) were observed with an accumulation of debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Ice machine was emptied and ice was disposed of. Slicer was taken apart and sanitized. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/30/2024

Various foods, which was previously cooked and cooled, was only reheated to 56-58 degrees Fahrenheit for hot holding. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food that is cooked, cooled, and reheated for hot holding must be reheated to 165 degrees Fahrenheit or above for 15 seconds. Food was moved to stove top to reheat. New Violation. Correct By: 04/30/2024
A knife was observed stored in between the prep units. Knife was relocated at the time of inpection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/30/2024
Rodent droppings were observed behind equipment in the bar area. Floor to be cleaned. New Violation. Correct By: 04/30/2024
